googleAuthFailed: Google sign-in failed. Please try again.
howToCreateWallet: How to set up a wallet
scanQrCode: Scan the QR code with your Lightning wallet
walletGuideSubtitle: Wallet of Satoshi — free, ready in 2 minutes
walletAndroid: Search for "Wallet of Satoshi" and install
walletIos: Search for "Wallet of Satoshi" and install

findAddressSteps.desc: Tap "Receive" in the bottom menu to see your Lightning address and QR code.
Tap the copy button next to your address (e.g. user@walletofsatoshi.com) to save it to your clipboard.
Paste the copied address in the sign-up screen or in Settings → Lightning address and you're done!

lightningAddressFormat: What a Lightning address looks like
lightningAddressFormatDesc: It looks just like an email — automatically assigned by the app, and the format is the same no matter which wallet you use.

stackHealth.title: Stack Health
stackHealth.body: Upload workout videos (5–30 seconds, up to 3 per day) to log your fitness and build a consistent workout habit with your community.

sweat.title: Sweat
sweat.body: You earn Sweat every time you upload a workout video. Sweat is reflected in challenge completions, leaderboard rankings, and other activity on the platform.

uploadPolicy.title: Upload Policy
uploadPolicy.body: Only upload workout videos you personally recorded. Inappropriate content will be removed immediately and your account will be suspended.

sweatConfirm.title: Sweat Confirmation
sweatConfirm.body: Sweat is confirmed 24 hours after upload. Deleting a video before it's confirmed will forfeit that Sweat. Sweat is an activity point on the platform and is not automatically converted to Bitcoin.

article1.title: Article 1 (Service Overview)
article1.body: Stack Health (the "Service") is a platform where users upload workout videos, share them with the community, and manage their fitness records.

article2.title: Article 2 (Sweat Points)
article2.body: Sweat on the Service is proof of your workout activity.

article3.title: Article 3 (Content Policy)
article3.body: Videos must be workout footage you personally recorded. Unauthorized uploads of others' content, explicit material, or violent content will result in immediate removal and account suspension.

article4.title: Article 4 (Disclaimer)
article4.body: The Service is not liable for service interruptions caused by external network failures, system maintenance, or other circumstances beyond its control.

architect.label: Architect
architect.description: Owns overall system design, component interfaces, and technical decisions. Distributes work to specialist agents.

backend.label: Backend
backend.description: Implements FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Pydantic. Receives API contracts from the architect and builds routes, services, and auth logic.

frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.description: Implements React + Vite + TailwindCSS + Zustand + TanStack Query. Receives designs from the architect and builds UI logic and API integrations.

dba.label: DBA
dba.description: Handles SQLAlchemy model design, Alembic migrations, and query optimization. Executes DB schema change requests from the architect.

devops.label: DevOps
devops.description: Handles Docker builds, deployment, environment variable management, and CI/CD. Executes infrastructure change requests from the architect.

planner.label: Planner
planner.description: Owns business priorities, roadmap alignment, scope management, and risk. Directly updates planning documents like SPEC.md.

designer.label: Designer
designer.description: Owns user experience, brand consistency, and channel-specific UX. Defines UX flows, component specs, and the design system, then hands off to the frontend team.

developer.label: Developer
developer.description: Assesses technical feasibility, implementation complexity, and stack fit. Delegates concrete implementation to specialist agents.

finance.label: Finance
finance.description: Handles cost structure, COGS, revenue and profit simulations, and break-even analysis.

marketing.label: Marketing
marketing.description: Handles channel strategy, customer acquisition, pricing, and competitive analysis.

ops.label: Operations
ops.description: Optimizes operational processes, designs SOPs, and assesses feasibility. Directly writes operational scripts and documentation.

qa.label: QA
qa.description: Owns validation metrics, failure scenarios, operational risk, and testability. Directly writes test code.

researcher.label: Researcher
researcher.description: Handles market research, literature review, and external data collection.

devil.label: Devil's Advocate
devil.description: Raises critical counterarguments against the direction of a discussion, surfacing overlooked risks, assumptions, and blind spots.
